import pytest

def test_trie_insert_search():
    # Get the insert and search functions
    insert, search = trie_insert_search()
    
    # Test basic functionality - happy path
    insert("hello")
    assert search("hello") == True
    assert search("hell") == False
    assert search("helloo") == False
    
    # Test multiple words
    insert("world")
    insert("help")
    assert search("hello") == True
    assert search("world") == True
    assert search("help") == True
    assert search("hel") == False
    
    # Test prefix vs complete word
    assert search("he") == False
    assert search("hel") == False
    assert search("hell") == False
    assert search("hello") == True
    
    # Test empty string
    insert("")
    assert search("") == True
    
    # Test overlapping words
    insert("he")
    assert search("he") == True
    assert search("hello") == True  # Should still work
    
    # Test case sensitivity
    insert("Hello")
    assert search("Hello") == True
    assert search("hello") == True  # This will actually be True because 'h' and 'H' are different
    
    # Test searching non-existent words
    assert search("xyz") == False
    assert search("helps") == False
    
    # Test inserting same word twice
    insert("test")
    assert search("test") == True
    insert("test")  # Insert again
    assert search("test") == True  # Should still work

def test_trie_edge_cases():
    insert, search = trie_insert_search()
    
    # Empty string edge cases
    assert search("") == False  # Before insertion
    insert("")
    assert search("") == True   # After insertion
    
    # Single character words
    insert("a")
    assert search("a") == True
    assert search("") == True   # Empty string still there
    
    # Very long word
    long_word = "a" * 1000
    insert(long_word)
    assert search(long_word) == True
    assert search(long_word[:-1]) == False
    
    # Words with special characters
    insert("hello-world")
    insert("hello_world")
    insert("hello123")
    assert search("hello-world") == True
    assert search("hello_world") == True
    assert search("hello123") == True

def test_trie_error_cases():
    insert, search = trie_insert_search()
    
    # Test search with None (if someone passes it)
    # Note: This would raise TypeError in the current implementation
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        search(None)
    
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        insert(None)
    
    # Test with non-string iterables
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        insert(123)
    
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        search(123)

# Additional test to verify trie structure isolation
def test_multiple_trie_instances():
    # Each call should create independent tries
    insert1, search1 = trie_insert_search()
    insert2, search2 = trie_insert_search()
    
    insert1("hello")
    assert search1("hello") == True
    assert search2("hello") == False  # Different trie instance
    
    insert2("world")
    assert search2("world") == True
    assert search1("world") == False  # Different trie instance

# Test to verify proper trie structure
def test_trie_structure():
    insert, search = trie_insert_search()
    
    # Insert related words to test shared prefixes
    insert("cat")
    insert("car")
    insert("card")
    
    # All should be found correctly
    assert search("cat") == True
    assert search("car") == True
    assert search("card") == True
    
    # Partial matches should not work
    assert search("ca") == False
    assert search("car") == True  # But full word should
    assert search("cards") == False

# Re-include the function for completeness
def trie_insert_search():
    trie = {}
    def insert(word):
        node = trie
        for c in word:
            node = node.setdefault(c, {})
        node['$'] = True
    def search(word):
        node = trie
        for c in word:
            if c not in node:
                return False
            node = node[c]
        return '$' in node
    return insert, search
